title: Закон за киберсигурност
description: Bulgaria's primary cybersecurity legislation transposing Directive (EU) 2016/1148 (NIS Directive) and subsequently updated to align with Directive (EU) 2022/2555 (NIS2). Establishes the national cybersecurity framework, CSIRT functions, incident reporting obligations, and security requirements for essential and important entities.

provisions.title: Subject and scope
provisions.content: This Act regulates the activities, principles, and management of cybersecurity in the Republic of Bulgaria. It implements the requirements of Directive (EU) 2016/1148 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 6 July 2016 concerning measures for a high common level of security of network and information systems across the Union (NIS Directive). The Act shall apply to operators of essential services, digital service providers, and the entities responsible for cybersecurity governance.

provisions.title: Definitions
provisions.content: For the purposes of this Act: 'cybersecurity' means the set of activities for protection of networks, information systems, users of these systems, and other persons affected by cyber threats; 'network and information systems' means electronic communications networks, devices or groups of interconnected or related devices that perform automatic processing of digital data, and digital data stored, processed, retrieved or transmitted by such systems; 'incident' means any event having an actual adverse effect on the security of network and information systems.

provisions.title: Principles of cybersecurity
provisions.content: The cybersecurity policy in the Republic of Bulgaria shall be based on the following principles: protection of fundamental rights and freedoms of citizens in cyberspace; ensuring the integrity, availability and confidentiality of network and information systems; risk-based approach to cybersecurity; cooperation and coordination among public authorities, private sector entities, and international partners; proportionality of cybersecurity measures to the identified risks.

provisions.title: National Cybersecurity Council
provisions.content: The Cybersecurity Council shall be the primary consultative and coordination body for cybersecurity policy in the Republic of Bulgaria. The Council shall be chaired by the Deputy Prime Minister responsible for public order and security, and shall include representatives of the Ministry of Electronic Governance, Ministry of Defence, State Agency for National Security, and other relevant authorities. The Council shall develop and propose the national cybersecurity strategy.

provisions.title: National competent authority
provisions.content: The national competent authority for the security of network and information systems shall be the Ministry of Electronic Governance acting through the State e-Government Agency. The competent authority shall supervise compliance with this Act and the measures adopted thereunder, designate operators of essential services, and serve as the national single point of contact within the meaning of Article 8 of Directive (EU) 2016/1148.

provisions.title: National CSIRT
provisions.content: The national Computer Security Incident Response Team (CSIRT) shall be established within the State e-Government Agency. The national CSIRT shall: monitor incidents at national level; provide early warning, alerts, announcements and dissemination of information to relevant stakeholders about risks and incidents; respond to incidents; provide dynamic risk and incident analysis; participate in the CSIRTs network established under Article 12 of Directive (EU) 2016/1148.

provisions.title: Sectoral CSIRTs
provisions.content: Sectoral Computer Security Incident Response Teams may be established by operators of essential services, government bodies, and critical infrastructure operators. Sectoral CSIRTs shall cooperate with the national CSIRT and share information on incidents and threats. The establishment and operation of sectoral CSIRTs shall be regulated by ordinance of the Council of Ministers.

provisions.title: Identification of operators
provisions.content: An entity shall be identified as an operator of essential services where: the entity provides a service which is essential for the maintenance of critical societal and economic activities; the provision of that service depends on network and information systems; an incident would have significant disruptive effects on the provision of that service. Essential services sectors include: energy, transport, banking, financial market infrastructures, health sector, drinking water supply and distribution, and digital infrastructure.

provisions.title: Security requirements
provisions.content: Operators of essential services shall take appropriate and proportionate technical and organisational measures to manage the risks posed to the security of the network and information systems which they use in their operations. These measures shall ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, taking into account the state of the art. Measures shall include: risk analysis and information security policies; incident handling procedures; business continuity management; supply chain security; security in network and information systems acquisition.

provisions.title: Incident notification
provisions.content: Operators of essential services shall notify the national CSIRT or the competent authority without undue delay of incidents having a significant impact on the continuity of the essential services they provide. The notification shall include information enabling determination of any cross-border impact of the incident. Notifications shall be made within 24 hours for early warning, within 72 hours for an incident notification, and within one month for a final report.

provisions.title: Significant impact criteria
provisions.content: The significance of the impact of an incident shall be determined by the following parameters: the number of users affected by the disruption of the essential service; the duration of the incident; the geographical spread with regard to the area affected by the incident; the extent of disruption of the functioning of the service; the extent of the impact on economic and societal activities.

provisions.title: Types of digital services
provisions.content: For the purposes of this Act, digital service providers include: providers of online marketplace services; providers of online search engine services; providers of cloud computing services. Digital service providers shall take appropriate and proportionate technical and organisational measures to manage the risks posed to the security of the network and information systems which they use to offer services within the European Union.

provisions.title: Security and notification for digital service providers
provisions.content: Digital service providers shall take measures to prevent and minimise the impact of incidents affecting the security of their network and information systems, with a view to ensuring the continuity of those services. When an incident has a substantial impact on the provision of a digital service, the provider shall notify the national CSIRT or the competent authority. Parameters for assessing substantial impact include: the number of users affected, the duration, geographical spread, the extent of disruption, and the impact on economic and societal activities.

provisions.title: Strategy development
provisions.content: The Council of Ministers shall adopt a national cybersecurity strategy upon proposal by the Cybersecurity Council. The strategy shall define the strategic objectives and appropriate policy and regulatory measures to achieve and maintain a high level of cybersecurity. The strategy shall address: governance framework and national cybersecurity objectives; identification of measures for preparedness, response and recovery; identification of relevant stakeholders; and an assessment of the cybersecurity risk landscape.

provisions.title: International cooperation
provisions.content: The Republic of Bulgaria shall cooperate with EU Member States and the European Union Agency for Cybersecurity (ENISA) on matters related to cybersecurity. The competent authority shall participate in the Cooperation Group established under Article 11 of Directive (EU) 2016/1148. Information shared within the Cooperation Group shall be treated in accordance with applicable rules on confidentiality and the protection of classified information.

provisions.title: Supervisory powers
provisions.content: The competent authority shall have the power to: require operators of essential services and digital service providers to provide the information necessary to assess the security of their network and information systems; require evidence of effective implementation of security policies; issue binding instructions to operators and providers regarding remedial measures; conduct security audits or require independent audits.

provisions.title: Administrative sanctions
provisions.content: An operator of essential services who fails to take the security measures required under Article 9 shall be subject to a fine of BGN 5,000 to BGN 25,000. An operator who fails to notify an incident in accordance with Article 10 shall be subject to a fine of BGN 1,000 to BGN 15,000. A digital service provider who violates the requirements under Article 12 or Article 13 shall be subject to a fine of BGN 1,000 to BGN 10,000. For repeated violations, the fine shall be doubled.

provisions.title: Liability
provisions.content: Operators of essential services and digital service providers shall be liable for damages caused by failure to comply with the security and notification obligations under this Act. The liability shall extend to damages suffered by third parties as a result of the incident, where the operator or provider has acted negligently. The State shall not be liable for damages caused by incidents in systems operated by private entities.

provisions.title: Implementation timeline
provisions.content: Existing operators of essential services shall comply with the requirements of this Act within six months of its entry into force. The Council of Ministers shall adopt the implementing ordinances required by this Act within twelve months of its entry into force. The national cybersecurity strategy shall be adopted within one year of the entry into force of this Act.

provisions.title: Alignment with NIS2
provisions.content: The provisions of this Act shall be interpreted and applied in conformity with Directive (EU) 2022/2555 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 14 December 2022 on measures for a high common level of cybersecurity across the Union (NIS2 Directive). The Council of Ministers shall adopt amendments necessary for full transposition of Directive (EU) 2022/2555 by October 2024.

definitions.definition: The set of activities for protection of networks, information systems, users of these systems, and other persons affected by cyber threats.
Any event having an actual adverse effect on the security of network and information systems.
An entity providing a service which is essential for the maintenance of critical societal and economic activities and depends on network and information systems.
